title: Settings
privacy_tracker_block_all_desc: Every remote resource in email bodies is blocked. Strongest protection; some newsletters may look broken.
privacy_tracker_block_known_desc: Tracking pixels and known tracker domains are blocked using the bundled ruleset. Recommended — this is the default.
privacy_tracker_allow_all_desc: No tracker blocking. Senders may learn when and where you open their email.
privacy_image_block_all_desc: Remote images never load automatically. You can load them per message. Recommended — this is the default.
privacy_image_trusted_only_desc: Images load automatically only for senders you have previously allowed.
privacy_image_allow_all_desc: Remote images always load. Senders may learn when and where you open their email.
privacy_reset_confirm_body: Tracking Protection returns to Block Known Trackers and Remote Image Loading returns to Block All.
privacy_immediate_note: Changes are applied immediately and stored locally on this device.
data_usage_desc: All mail, attachments, and the search index are stored on this device.
data_link_export_desc: Export stored mail per account as mbox or JSON Lines, packaged as a ZIP bundle.
data_link_reindex_desc: Rebuild the semantic and keyword index from stored mail. Resumable if interrupted.
data_link_sync_range_desc: Choose how much mail history each account keeps on this device.
data_link_data_flow_desc: See exactly where each category of your data goes — and what never leaves this device.
data_link_wipe_desc: Permanently remove an account's local mail, attachments, and index entries.
wipe_scope_mails_desc: Removes mail, threads, and attachments from this device. The search index is untouched.
wipe_scope_mails_index_desc: Also removes this account's entries from the semantic and keyword index.
wipe_scope_everything_desc: Removes the account configuration too. The last remaining account cannot be removed.
wipe_irreversible: This cannot be undone. Mail on your mail server is not affected.
wipe_confirm_body: To confirm you understand this permanently removes the selected local data, type DELETE (in capitals) below.
reindex_confirm_body: SeekerMail re-reads every stored message and rebuilds the semantic and keyword index. For large mailboxes this can take a while.
reindex_sync_paused_note: New mail is not synced while the rebuild runs. Syncing resumes automatically afterwards.
reindex_cancel_note: Cancelling keeps a checkpoint — the next rebuild resumes where this one stopped.
sync_range_grow_started: Range extended — older mail is being fetched in the background.
sync_range_shrink_body: {count, plural, one {# locally stored message falls} other {# locally stored messages fall}} outside the new range and will be removed from this device. Mail on your server is not affected.
sync_range_shrink_done: {count, plural, one {# message removed} other {# messages removed}} from this device.
sync_range_note: Growing the range fetches older history from your mail server. Shrinking removes local copies only.
data_flow_intro: Where your data goes under the current configuration. SeekerMail is local-first: mail content, metadata, and the search index never leave this device.
data_flow_dest_local_device: Stored on this device. Never leaves it.
data_flow_dest_your_server: Your configured mail server — controlled by you.
data_flow_dest_seekermail: updates.seekermail.app — sends app version and platform only. No mail or account data.
data_flow_footer: Allowed outbound destinations are limited to your configured mail servers and updates.seekermail.app — nothing else.
data_flow_no_seekermail_proxy: Your AI requests go directly to your chosen provider. SeekerMail servers are never in the path.
data_flow_ai_cloud_note: Mail content is sent to this endpoint when AI runs for this account.
data_flow_ai_disabled: AI disabled for this account — mail content stays on this device.
data_flow_ai_no_activity: No AI requests in the last 24 hours.
export_date_range_desc: Leave both fields empty to export the full history.
export_format_mbox_desc: One mbox file per account (RFC 4155). Opens in most mail clients and archive tools.
export_format_json_desc: One JSON Lines file per account plus a MANIFEST.json. Best for scripts and data pipelines.
export_credentials_note: Account passwords and API keys are never included in exports.
export_complete_count: {count, plural, one {# message exported.} other {# messages exported.}}
export_disk_full: Not enough free disk space for this export. Free up space or narrow the date range.
export_failed: The export stopped because of an error. Check the log for details.
ai_providers_desc: Connect your own AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, or a local model) to power each account's agent. Keys are stored in the system Keychain and never leave this device.
ai_coming_soon: Provider configuration — coming in a later version.
about_tagline: A local-first mail client where every account is a digital employee.
about_built_with: Built with Tauri, Rust, and React.
about_local_first: Your mail stays on your device — always.
onboarding_subtitle: Add your first account to begin. Everything stays on your device.
onboarding_welcome_title: Your email, powered by AI agents
onboarding_welcome_subtitle: SeekerMail treats each account as a digital employee — a local AI agent that reads, drafts, and acts on your behalf. Everything runs on your device. Nothing leaves without your approval.
onboarding_add_account_subtitle: Connect an email account to get started. SeekerMail supports IMAP / SMTP and OAuth (Google, Microsoft).
onboarding_knowledge_depth_title: How far back should your agent learn?
onboarding_knowledge_depth_subtitle: Your agent builds its context from your email history. Choose how much history to sync.
onboarding_depth_3m_desc: Fast sync — recommended for getting started quickly.
onboarding_depth_1y_desc: Balanced — good context without a lengthy initial sync.
onboarding_depth_all_desc: Full context. Initial sync may take a while for large mailboxes.
onboarding_ready_subtitle: SeekerMail is syncing your mail in the background. Your agent will be ready to assist shortly.
list_empty_unread: All caught up — no unread messages.
